cards.back: A comparison-based sorting algorithm that uses arrays of counters to count the occurrences of each element, making it less dependent on comparison operations

cards.front: What are the advantages of Comparison Counting Sort?
cards.back: It performs well for nearly sorted arrays and has a best-case time complexity of O(n), making it suitable for certain scenarios

cards.front: What is the space complexity of Comparison Counting Sort?
cards.back: The space complexity is O(n), as it requires additional space to store the counters

cards.front: Is Comparison Counting Sort suitable for sorting large datasets?
cards.back: It is not as efficient as other comparison-based sorting algorithms like QuickSort or MergeSort for large datasets due to its higher overhead

cards.front: What is the average-case time complexity of Comparison Counting Sort?
cards.back: It is between O(n) and O(n log n), depending on the distribution of the input data
